mechanism_by_architecture.cot: The L858R mutation in EGFR leads to constitutive activation of the receptor, promoting uncontrolled cell growth and proliferation. This mutation is particularly sensitive to tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs) that target the mutated EGFR.

mechanism_by_architecture.single: T790M gatekeeper mutation sterically blocks first- and second-generation EGFR TKIs while retaining sensitivity to third-generation osimertinib.
mechanism_by_architecture.cot: Gatekeeper T790M increases bulk at the ATP pocket, conferring resistance to gefitinib/erlotinib/afatinib; osimertinib retains binding via covalent C797 targeting.
mechanism_by_architecture.blackboard: Blackboard consensus: T790M gatekeeper blocks 1st/2nd gen TKIs; osimertinib recommended for T790M-positive NSCLC.

rows.mechanism_preview: T790M gatekeeper mutation sterically blocks first- and second-generation EGFR TKIs while retaining sensitivity to third-generation osimertinib.
Gatekeeper T790M increases bulk at the ATP pocket, conferring resistance to gefitinib/erlotinib/afatinib; osimertinib retains binding via covalent C797 targeting.
Blackboard consensus: T790M gatekeeper blocks 1st/2nd gen TKIs; osimertinib recommended for T790M-positive NSCLC.

mechanism_by_architecture.cot: The E545K mutation in the activation loop of PIK3CA leads to constitutive activation of the PI3K pathway, which is a key driver in breast cancer. This mutation enhances sensitivity to PI3K inhibitors like Alpelisib.

mechanism_by_architecture.cot: The R175H mutation destabilizes the p53 DNA-binding domain.
mechanism_by_architecture.blackboard: {'summary': "The R175H mutation in TP53 affects the DNA-binding surface of the protein, specifically in the beta-sandwich core region. The high pLDDT score at the residue (96.6) and the significant local contact density (25) indicate that the mutation has a substantial impact on the protein's structure.
